g.text: State Store — PostgreSQL JSONB Key-Value Engine
Per-butler schema isolation · Async CRUD · CAS for optimistic concurrency · Integer version counter
version: INTEGER starting at 1, increments on every write
(used as CAS token for optimistic concurrency)
2. Key absent: CAS on non-existent key
val, ver = state_get(...) # read + note version
DATA FLOW — NUMBERED SEQUENCE (MCP Tool → State Store → PostgreSQL)
2. SELECT value FROM state WHERE key = $1
2. SELECT value FROM state WHERE key = $1
Step 2. UPDATE state SET value=$3, version=version+1
WHERE key=$1 AND version=$2 RETURNING version
Step 2. UPDATE state SET value=$3, version=version+1
WHERE key=$1 AND version=$2 RETURNING version
Step 3 (conflict path): 0 rows affected → SELECT version to diagnose → raise CASConflictError(key, expected, actual)
Step 3 (success path): 1 row affected → returns new version integer
Step 3 (success path): 1 row affected → returns new version integer
Step 4: returns new_version to MCP tool | raises CASConflictError
Step 4: returns new_version to MCP tool | raises CASConflictError

to butler_alpha schema. It cannot issue
